name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-55
title: test-scope-guard-centralized.py: t1/t2 use weak substring matching ('bugs' in sg / 'backlogs' in sg)
description: test/test-scope-guard-centralized.py lines 47-56: checks 'if "bugs" in sg' and 'if "backlogs" in sg' against scope-guard.py source. Any unrelated comment or variable name containing those substrings (e.g., the docstring 'no scope marker') would falsely satisfy the assertion. The current source happens to contain '.claude/bugs/' and '.claude/backlogs/' literally, so the tests pass for the right reason, but the assertion is not precise. Strengthen to check the exact path-prefix tuple ('/.claude/bugs/' and '/.claude/backlogs/').
